L1.area: Audio file loading and decompression pipeline

L2.description: A double free vulnerability exists where dynamically allocated memory for song data is explicitly deallocated in an error-handling path, but the class destructor also unconditionally deallocates the same memory, resulting in the memory being freed twice when decompression of a crafted audio file fails.

L3.area: Ultima 6 music format file loading and LZW decompression handling
L3.description: A double free vulnerability exists in the Ultima 6 music format player where dynamically allocated memory for song data is explicitly deallocated in the error-handling path when LZW decompression fails, but the class destructor also unconditionally deallocates the same memory, resulting in the memory being freed twice when processing a crafted audio file.

ground_truth.reason: When LZW decompression fails in the load function, song_data is explicitly deleted in the error path, but the destructor also deletes song_data unconditionally, causing a double free.
